title: The QED tombstone ∎ — a single filled square marking the end of proof. Precept's compiler closes the proof before runtime: the definition is verified, every invariant holds, no invalid configuration exists. ∎. The icon is that mark — one square, the proof complete. Unlike 27-proof-mark (nested rectangles with outer frame), this is just the tombstone alone: maximum simplicity, maximum certainty.
conceptualBridge: The tombstone square means Precept's compile-time proof is complete because ∎ is the mathematical mark that ends a verified argument — the definition has been proven sound and nothing more is left to check.
decision: Rejected: distinct silhouette hard fail (2.5 < 3 minimum) — a single centered square is the most generic icon shape possible; without the outer frame of 27-proof-mark, the QED reading collapses and the form reads as any app icon or square logo. The nested-rectangle 27-proof-mark is the correct representation of this concept.
